title: Minimum Wage Schedule 2024: Delhi
content: Under the Minimum Wages Act 1948, the state government of Delhi sets the statutory minimum wage for scheduled employments. As of the 2024 notification, an unskilled worker must receive at least ₹17494 per month. A skilled or highly skilled worker must receive at least ₹21215 per month, excluding discretionary allowances. Paying below these rates violates Section 22 of the Minimum Wages Act and is a criminal offence.

title: Professional Tax: Delhi (No PT Levied)
content: Delhi has not enacted a Professional Tax law. Employers operating in Delhi must not deduct any Professional Tax from employees' salaries. Any such deduction would be unlawful.

title: Minimum Wage Schedule 2024: Maharashtra
content: Under the Minimum Wages Act 1948, the state government of Maharashtra sets the statutory minimum wage for scheduled employments. As of the 2024 notification, an unskilled worker must receive at least ₹14000 per month. A skilled or highly skilled worker must receive at least ₹16000 per month, excluding discretionary allowances. Paying below these rates violates Section 22 of the Minimum Wages Act and is a criminal offence.

title: Professional Tax Schedule: Maharashtra
content: Maharashtra levies Professional Tax under Article 276(2) of the Constitution. The maximum annual ceiling is ₹2,500. Monthly deduction slabs are: Salary ≤7,500: ₹0/month. Salary 7,501–10,000: ₹175/month. Salary >10,000: ₹200/month. Employers must deduct PT from each employee's salary and remit to the state government. Failure to deduct or remit PT is an offence under the state PT Act.

title: EPF Contribution Rate — Section 6
content: Under Section 6 of the Employees' Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act 1952, both the employee and employer must each contribute 12% of the employee's basic wages. Of the employer's 12%, 8.33% is diverted to the Employees' Pension Scheme (EPS) and 3.67% goes to the EPF account. The wage ceiling for mandatory EPF/EPS contributions is ₹15,000 per month.

title: EPF Wage Ceiling — Para 26(6)
content: The statutory wage ceiling for EPF/EPS contributions is ₹15,000 per month. Employers are not required to contribute on wages above ₹15,000 unless a joint option under Para 26(6) is exercised. Employees earning above ₹15,000 may voluntarily opt to contribute on their full wages by submitting a joint request with their employer within 6 months of joining.

title: ESI Wage Ceiling and Contribution Rates
content: The Employees' State Insurance (ESI) scheme applies to employees whose gross salary is ₹21,000 per month or less (₹25,000 for employees with disabilities). The ESI contribution rates are: employee 0.75% of gross wages, employer 3.25% of gross wages. ESI applies to establishments with 10 or more employees (20 in some states). Employees earning above ₹21,000 gross are not covered by ESI.

title: Code on Wages 2019 — Wage Definition and 50% Basic Rule
content: Under Section 2(y) of the Code on Wages 2019, 'wages' includes basic pay, dearness allowance, and retaining allowance. The Code requires that the Basic Pay component must constitute at least 50% of the total gross salary. If an employer structures the salary such that Basic Pay falls below 50% of gross, the excess allowances are treated as wages for EPF/ESI computation purposes. This prevents employers from artificially suppressing PF contributions.

title: Payment of Bonus Act 1965 — Eligibility and Calculation
content: Under the Payment of Bonus Act 1965, every employee earning up to ₹21,000 per month is eligible for a statutory bonus. The minimum bonus is 8.33% of annual basic salary (or ₹100, whichever is higher). The maximum bonus payable is 20% of annual basic salary. Employees whose basic salary exceeds ₹7,000 per month or the minimum wage (whichever is higher) have the bonus calculated on ₹7,000 for the purposes of the Act. The employer must pay bonus within 8 months of the close of the accounting year.
